role_delineation_check: The Social Worker manages the clinical discharge plan and coordinates with the home health agency, while the Navigator focuses on verifying the patient's demographic data in Atlantis, educating the family on the Healing Partners program, and flagging any transition anxiety to the clinical team.
narrative_summary: Margaret, a 72-year-old recovering from a hip fracture and subsequent surgical fixation, was progressing well toward a home discharge until a breakdown in communication left the transition plan in flux. Although the facility held a final planning meeting without the Navigator present, Margaret’s family was left confused about the timing of her home health arrival and the role of the support team. The Navigator bridged this gap by proactively seeking a verbal update from the Social Worker and then clarifying the first-day expectations with the family to ensure a seamless return home.
demographic_audit_note: The Navigator compared the facility face sheet to the Atlantis record and found that Margaret's primary phone number was missing a digit; the correction was made in Atlantis to ensure the home team could reach her upon discharge.
home_vs_ltc_goal: Home with Home Health Agency support
v_card_flyer_status: The Navigator helped Margaret's daughter save the Healing Partners contact information as a V-Card so she would recognize the caller ID and provided a flyer explaining the Medical Assistant's role during the first twenty-four hours at home.
pre_dc_pulse_call: During the twenty-four-hour check-in, Margaret's daughter confirmed they were prepared for the transition, though she expressed some minor nerves about the timing of the first nursing visit which were noted for the Social Worker.
atlantis_final_sync: Once the Social Worker verbally confirmed the home health agency was in place, the Navigator logged the discharge date and the scheduled time for the Medical Assistant's first visit in Atlantis.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The Navigator introduced the Healing Partners program to Margaret and her daughter, explaining how the Medical Assistant supports the first day home; the family expressed interest in the added layer of monitoring.

The Social Worker held a transition huddle with the physical therapy team and the family but the Navigator was not notified or invited; this resulted in a lack of clarity regarding the confirmed home health provider.

After learning the meeting had occurred, the Navigator asked the Social Worker for a verbal status update; the Social Worker confirmed that a local agency had accepted the case for a Tuesday start of care.
With the agency confirmed, the Navigator updated Atlantis with the discharge details and scheduled the Medical Assistant to visit Margaret within the first twenty-four hours of her arrival home.

format_2_triples.situation: The Navigator was excluded from the final discharge planning huddle and lacked the updated transition timeline.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator requested a brief verbal summary from the Social Worker to align on the confirmed agency and discharge date.

format_2_triples.situation: The family expressed confusion about who would be visiting the home on the first day following the surgical hip repair.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator explained the specific role of the Medical Assistant and how they coordinate with the nursing team during the transition window.

format_2_triples.situation: During the demographic audit, the Navigator noticed the patient's secondary contact person was not listed in the internal records.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator alerted the Social Worker to the missing contact information and updated the patient's profile in Atlantis for future coordination.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: After realizing a discharge meeting took place without their presence, the Navigator documents the communication gap in Atlantis and waits for the Social Worker to send a written update or call with the new details.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the Social Worker's ownership of the discharge process and ensures the Navigator does not act on incomplete or unverified information.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator approaches the Social Worker directly to ask for a verbal update on the discharge timeline and the name of the assigned home health agency, then documents these details in Atlantis.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This allows the Navigator to stay informed and fulfill their role as a liaison without overstepping into clinical or vendor management.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator calls the home health agency's intake department to confirm if they have Margaret's referral and to ask for the name of the nurse who will be assigned to the first visit.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This violates the no-vendor rule and interferes with the Social Worker's responsibility to manage the agency relationship and referral process.
